description: Outbound play templates. Each play is selected by (primary_signal × persona × tier). Body templates use {{handlebars}} placeholders that src/outbound/plays.py fills from the lead + signal payload.

plays.subject: Congrats on the {{round}} — quick thought on creative scale
plays.opener: Saw {{company}} closed a {{round}} ${{amount_m}}M last month. Most marketing teams I work with hit a creative-throughput wall ~6 months after a round like this — variants pile up, brand consistency cracks. Worth 15 min to compare notes on how Sweetgreen / Peloton handled it?

plays.subject: Post-{{round}} creative ops — {{company}}
plays.opener: Congrats on the {{round}}. The story I keep hearing from CDs after a raise: the new headcount triples your asset library overnight, and tracking what's approved-to-ship gets dicey by month 3. Curious how you're set up — happy to share what {{reference_brand}} does.

plays.subject: Welcome / quick intro for the new chapter at {{company}}
plays.opener: Saw {{exec_name}} just joined as {{exec_title}}. New marketing leaders almost always inherit a creative library in disarray — usually one of the first 90-day fixes. Wanted to put Air on your radar early; happy to skip the demo and just send a Loom of how {{reference_brand}} runs theirs.

plays.subject: Congrats on the {{exec_title}} role at {{company}}
plays.opener: Welcome to the seat. Most CDs I talk to in their first 60 days at a new shop are auditing the asset library and finding 3+ tools doing fragments of the job. Air is what most {{vertical}} teams consolidate to — happy to share their migration playbook if useful.

plays.subject: {{competitor}} renewal coming up at {{company}}?
plays.opener: Most teams renewing {{competitor}} this year are quietly mapping alternatives — usually because of search/findability, AI tagging, or pricing. We've migrated {{rep_competitor_wins}} {{competitor}} customers in the last 18 months. Want a no-pitch comparison doc?

plays.subject: {{company}} is staffing up creative — saw {{role_count}} roles open
plays.opener: Noticed {{role_count}} creative roles at {{company}} — designer, social, video. Onboarding new creatives without a single source of truth is where the wheels come off. We help teams structure the library before the new hires start, not after. 15 min to walk through it?

plays.subject: Agency transition at {{company}} — asset handoff thoughts
plays.opener: Saw the {{from_agency}} → {{to_agency}} switch. Asset handoffs in agency transitions are where 6-12 months of work gets lost or mis-versioned. We're the system most brands use to own their library through the transition so the new agency starts with structure, not chaos. Worth a chat?

plays.subject: {{company}}'s {{campaign_or_rebrand}} — single source of truth
plays.opener: Loved the {{campaign_or_rebrand}}. The hardest part of any launch like that isn't shipping it, it's keeping every team — paid, social, retail, sales enablement — using the right approved assets through the long tail. That's the exact problem Air solves. Open to a 15-min walkthrough?

plays.subject: {{vertical}} brands & creative ops
plays.opener: Reaching out because we work with a lot of {{vertical}} teams at {{company_size_band}} — {{reference_brand}}, etc. Most have the same creative library pain (versioning, brand consistency, channel-sized variants). 15-min chat to see if it resonates for {{company}}?

plays.subject: Quick one for {{company}}
plays.opener: Founder-to-founder mode: most {{vertical}} teams under 100 people are juggling Drive + Slack + Frame for creative. Air replaces the whole stack for $X/mo. 10-min Loom if you want it?
